untranslatable_reason: Diamond pattern detection requires multi-bar price shape recognition (symmetrical diamond formation across 4+ bars). This cannot be expressed using point-in-time indicator comparisons. The pattern requires visual/geometric analysis of price structure that exceeds the capabilities of the allowed operators and indicators. Entry conditions specify 'Diamond formation identified in price chart' which requires pattern recognition logic not available in simple conditional rules.
translation_notes: Signal describes a technical chart pattern (Diamond) that is inherently visual and geometric in nature. While breakout direction (upward vs downward) could theoretically be captured by price crossing pivot levels, the core pattern identification requirement—detecting the symmetrical diamond shape itself—cannot be translated into standard indicator-based rules. Bulkowski's data (140 up-breakouts in bull markets, 63 down-breakouts, etc.) are statistical frequencies, not entry/exit triggers. The 36% average rise and 21% average decline are performance metrics, not actionable conditions. Recommend implementing via dedicated pattern recognition module or candle pattern library rather than conditional rule engine.
